document_title: Laser Notice No. 50 - Conformance with IEC 60825-1 and IEC 60601-2-22
supersedes: Laser Products - Conformance with IEC 60825-1, Am. 2 and IEC 60601-2-22; Final Guidance (July 26, 2001)
regulatory_authority: FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH)

executive_summary.purpose: Describes conditions under which laser product manufacturers may introduce IEC-compliant products into U.S. commerce
executive_summary.legal_status: Nonbinding recommendations - does not create or confer rights
executive_summary.key_concept: Harmonization of FDA requirements with IEC standards
executive_summary.effectiveness_end: On effective date(s) of amendments to Federal regulations

contact_information.name: Jerome Dennis
contact_information.address: Office of Communication, Education, and Radiation Programs (HFZ-240), Center for Devices and Radiological Health, 1350 Piccard Drive, Rockville, MD 20850

mandatory_requirements.introduction: Laser products for introduction into commerce in or imported into the United States must comply with:

harmonized_sections.introduction: CDRH will not object to conformance with comparable sections of IEC 60825-1 (Editions 1.2 or 2) or 60601-2-22 in lieu of FDA requirements for:
harmonized_sections.note: CDRH intends to amend federal regulations to reflect harmonization with these IEC sections

unaffected_sections.introduction: The following sections remain unaffected - laser products MUST conform to these FDA requirements:

sections.reason: Beyond IEC scope or sufficiently different
Beyond IEC scope or sufficiently different

sections.description: Surveying, leveling and alignment laser products

unaffected_sections.rationale: These requirements are either beyond IEC scope, sufficiently different, or are not normative and included as recommendations in IEC

previous_concessions.description: CDRH has previously issued notices stating non-objection to:
concessions: Lack of emission indicators or beam attenuators on Class II and Class IIIa systems
Hazard warning labels as specified in IEC 60825-1

modified_certification_statement.requirement: Manufacturers must use modified compliance statement on certification label
options: Complies with FDA performance standards for laser products except for deviations pursuant to Laser Notice No. 50, dated (Insert date of this guidance.)
Complies with 21 CFR 1040.10 and 1040.11 except for deviations pursuant to Laser Notice No. 50, dated June 24, 2007

reporting_requirements.requirement: Submit product reports or supplemental reports to describe changes to products in accordance with this guidance

key_definitions.least_burdensome_approach: FDA should consider least burdensome approach in all areas of medical device and electronic product regulation
key_definitions.guidance_interpretation: The word 'should' in Agency guidances means suggested or recommended, but not required
key_definitions.alternative_approach: Alternative approaches are acceptable if they satisfy applicable statutes and regulations

iec_standards_referenced.title: Safety of laser products - Part 1: Equipment classification and requirements
Medical electrical equipment - Part 2-22: Particular requirements for basic safety and essential performance of surgical, cosmetic, therapeutic and diagnostic laser equipment

action_items_for_manufacturers: Determine if product can comply with IEC standards for harmonized sections
Ensure compliance with unaffected FDA-specific sections
Update certification label with modified statement
Submit product reports or supplemental reports describing IEC conformance

additional_resources.public_comments_address: Division of Dockets Management, Food and Drug Administration, 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1061, (HFA-305), Rockville, MD 20852
